evidenceSnippets: : RICHARD N. HAASS FROM: C. DAVID WELCH SUBJECT: Letter from John Temple Swing, Council on Foreign Relations, Regarding Middle East Peace Project John Temple Swing wrote to you on January 31 (Tab II) concerning the Council on Foreign Relations Middle East Peace Project that he has been working on, along with Gidon Gottlieb. He and Gidon have, in the meantime, met with Richar

and discussed their project. The CFR Middle East Project is an academic approach to the problems of peace-making in the Arab-Israeli conflict. It has limited applicability to the peace process we have underway, though its value is probably greater in the psychological dimension of breaking down barriers to understanding and communication. We have prepared a brief acknow

s historic milestone. A meeting of the American, Israeli and Egyptian foreign ministers, leading to the convening of an Israeli-Palestinian dialogue in Cairo, is within our grasp. --Our goal remains what is has always been: a dialogue on elections and the negotiating process, leading to negotiations on transitional arrangements and final status. I would hope that Israel would

the period that must precede such negotiations. We have, in hand, drafted materials outlining steps consistent with the Shamir initiative that, we are led to believe, the PLO may be willing to consider for the period that would follow the Cairo talks, should they ever take place.
